growth_records.medium: Optimised LMO-812 (defined marine medium) + 20 mM glucose

mfa.note: EDP dominant but WP3 uniquely runs EDP+PPP+EMPP in parallel
lowest EDP cleavage of the four = highest PPP diversion
PPP flux ~25%; non-oxidative PPP = 14.4/25.1 = 57.4% of total PPP input (unique to WP3)

growth_records.cite: Yuxue Yang et al. Metabolic Flux Analysis Reveals Entner-Doudoroff Pathway Dominance in Heterotrophic Deep-Sea Bacterial Isolates. Environmental Microbiology Reports (2026). DOI:10.1111/1758-2229.70379. [PMC13261371]
growth_records.method: reported (max specific growth rate from OD600, 0.059 +/- 0.003 /h); td CALCULATED=0.693/0.059=11.75 h
growth_records.snippet: 0.059 +/- 0.003 h-1 ... WP3 0.77 +/- 0.09 (Glucose consumption umol/h) ... Biomass yield 0.20 +/- 0.02 ... the non-oxidative PPP flux accounted for more than 50% of the total PPP input flux (14.4/25.1 = 57.4%)
growth_records.notes: mu 0.059/h; td=0.693/0.059=11.75 h. Glucose consumption 0.77 umol/h (per culture, Table 2). Yxs=0.20 g CDW/g glucose. WP3 distinct: substantial oxidative AND non-oxidative PPP (RU5P from 6PG 25.1%; non-ox PPP 57.4% of PPP input per paper arithmetic 14.4/25.1). Temp 20C, glucose 20 mM. Harvest 65 h, OD600 0.15. No secretion products quantified.
